steps.title: Find the key signal in the conclusion
steps.content: The team concludes: “the younger the dog, the worse it may be at differentiating between familiar and unfamiliar languages.” Any supporting finding must show that younger dogs exhibit a smaller or weaker difference in neural response when comparing known versus unknown languages.

steps.title: Match the evidence to that signal
steps.content:
The correct response directly demonstrates this requirement: “the difference in brain activity decreased as the age of the dog scanned decreased” means younger dogs show smaller neural distinctions between languages they know and don't know, proving weaker differentiation.

The other choices either show all ages respond identically, measure overall activity levels rather than language-specific differences or examine the wrong comparison—scrambled speech versus unfamiliar language.

That confirms B is correct.
